rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WILLIAM JACKSON BROWN JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS WILLIAM BROWN, GARY NIDUAZA AND KIM DOBESH'S Motion To Compel The petition to compel arbitration is granted. The Court nominates the following individuals as arbitrators from the Court's list of arbitrators: (1) Neil Bardack; (2) Susan Keeney; (3) Dennis Isaac; (4) Nancy E. Hudgins; (5) James Hannawalt. The parties are to meet and confer to determine whether they can decide on an arbitrator in compliance with CCP 1281.6. If the parties cannot agree within the 5-day period of CCP 1281.6, then petitioner may make an ex parte application for the appointment of an arbitrator. The Court will not set a deadline for the arbitration to be complete. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

On November 15, 2013, Defendant Unger served second supplemental and amended to Special Interrogatories, Set One as requested by Plaintiff. rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA VS. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Pltff To Provide Further Responses To Deft'S Firt Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Defendant's First Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to advise as to status of further discovery responses by Plaintiff. If no responses have been filed, motions to compel will be granted. If responses have been filed, parties to address how the court should proceed. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC VS. KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, KOHL'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC., A DELAWARE AND KOHL'S ILLINOIS, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings All requests for judicial notice are granted. The Court has considered both supplemental briefs filed. Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. Res judicata applies, as all three prongs are met. (Lyons v. Security Pacific Nat. Bank (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1001, 1005.) First, there was a final judgment on the merits in John Moore v. Benson Mills, Inc., et al. (Santa Clara Superior Court Case No. 112CV237078). "A court-approved settlement acts as a final judgment on the merits for the purposes of res judicata. A judgment entered ... by consent or stipulation, is as conclusive a ... bar as a judgment rendered after trial." (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694, citations and quotations omitted.) Second, the party against whom res judicata is raised was in privity with the party to the prior adjudication. Citizens acting in the public interest as private attorney generals are in privity with other citizen enforcers of that same right. (Id. at 689-93.) Third, the issues decided in the Moore action are the same as those presented in this action. The Consent Judgment in the Moore action explicitly includes, and amends the Moore complaint to include "vinyl placemats that bear a brand or trademark owned or licensed by Kohl's or affiliated entity that are sold or offered for sale by Kohl's in the State of California." (See Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 3, Consent Judgment, paras. 1.6 "Complaint shall be deemed amended to include all Covered Products as defined in this Consent Judgment¿; para. 2.1.1 defining "Covered Products.") The two placemats identified in the Complaint and 60-Day Notices in this action bear a brand or trademark owned or licensed by Kohl's and therefore the claims pertaining to them are released pursuant to Section 6.1 of the Moore Consent Judgment. (Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 3, Consent Judgment, para. 6.1; Defendant's Supplemental Request for Judicial Notice, Exs. 1 and 2.) Because the only two products alleged in the Complaint in this action have been released by the Moore Consent Judgment, there are no remaining allegations in the Complaint. (See generally Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. Kinetsu (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 953.) Additionally, Plaintiff has not established that the narrow public interest exception to res judicata should apply here. (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER CALVI VS. VITO J. LBERATI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 13(g); "callously and recklessly" from paragraph 14; "reckless" from paragraph 21, and paragraph 5 on page 9 (in prayer for relief) is denied. See Baptiste v. Superior Court (1980) 106 Cal.App.3d 87, 96, 100, 104 (Jones Act); Atlantic Sounding Co. Inc. v. Townsend (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 415 (unseaworthiness); Evich v. Morris (9th Cir. 1987) 819 F.2d 256, 258 ("Punitive damages are available under general maritime law for claims of unseaworthiness"); Rowe v. Hornblower Fleet (N.D.Cal. 2012) 2012 WL 5833541 ("no provisions in the Jones Act that limit the right to seek punitive damages on a claim for unseaworthiness"). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrgoatories Propounded To Defts Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrgatories Propounded To Defendants Request For Monetary Sanctions Denied. (CCP § 2030.060(d).) The Special Interrogatories at issue do not comply with CCP § 2030.060(d) as each one is not "full and complete in and of itself." Further, the Special Interrogatories require the answering party to refer to outside materials which is improper. (Catanese v. Sup.Ct. (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1159, 1164-1165.) Lastly, the special interrogatories are compound. (CCP § 2030.060(f). See, e.g. Special Interrogatories, no. 1). Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA DDS VS. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF ASHWIN NANDA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Continued to February 11, 2014 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard at the same time as Defendant's motion for summary judgment. rulings.Case Title: SHERRI CRESPIN VS. ARAMARK CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Filing Of Complaint In Intervention;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 15, INTERVENOR HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE MIDWEST'S Motion For Order Permitting Filing Of Complaint In Intervention; Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SEMEYON GOFMAN VS. WHITTIER ENTERPRISE LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Attorney Fees As Prevailing Party
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 16, PLAINTIFF SEMEYON GOFMAN'S MOTION For Attorney Fees As Prevailing Party Continued to February 3, 2014, to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 8, 2014, at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE OCENAS VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE DBA ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2) And Request For Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE'S Motion To Compel Arbitration (Ccp 1281.2) And Request For Stay Of Proceedings Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The Court will sign the proposed Order submitted with the moving papers. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: BERNARDA LIGGETT VS. SAUSE BROS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANT SAUSE BROS, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff may maintain the unseaworthiness/loss of consortium cause of action under the reasoning of Barrette v. Jubilee Fisheries, Inc. (WD Wa. 2011) 2011 U.S. Dist. Lexis 89514. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. FACTORY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT FACTORY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE Off calendar as untimely under CCP § 1005(b). Moving party did not give opposing party 16 court days notice. The proof of service indicates the motion was served by overnight courier on December 19, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM
